If there is a fit, Rafał stays accountable from the first scope to delivery.

featured.label: Start with the work
featured.title: If you want to know whether AwesomeWorks can help, start with the work, not the promise.
featured.description: Browse the shipped products, see the range, and decide whether you want help building a system, improving one, or learning the workflow inside your own team.

items.title: CallWise shows what strong sales execution looks like
items.description: You can see how AI becomes a repeatable coaching system inside a real sales process.

items.title: CourseAI shows how teams build faster in-house
items.description: If you want the workflow behind this kind of execution, CourseAI turns it into a practical system you can apply inside your own team.

items.title: Ask and LLM Goose show operations and engineering depth
items.description: From finance workflows to agent testing, the work goes beyond demos and into the systems teams actually depend on.

problems.title: Most AI projects do not fail because of models. They fail because of handoffs, fuzzy scope, and unclear ownership.
problems.subtitle: The companies that reach out to AwesomeWorks usually do not need more inspiration. They need sharper decisions, fewer layers, and someone who can turn ambiguity into a shipped system.

items.title: Too many conversations, not enough movement
items.description: Strategy happens in one room, technical reality in another, and implementation gets delayed between agencies, freelancers, and internal teams.

items.title: AI gets treated like a demo instead of an operating system
items.description: Teams prototype something clever, but it never becomes part of the workflow, revenue motion, or day-to-day operations.

items.title: No one owns the decision from first call to launch
items.description: By the time scope reaches delivery, context is diluted. That creates rework, weak prioritization, and avoidable surprises.

items.title: Buyers want clarity before they want a proposal
items.description: The first conversation should help them understand what is worth building now, what can wait, and what outcome is realistic.

workflow.title: One workflow for clarity, delivery, and adoption
workflow.subtitle: AwesomeWorks combines strategic framing, product thinking, and hands-on implementation so the work does not dissolve between separate vendors or layers of account management.

steps.title: Clarity call with Rafał
steps.description: We unpack the business problem, current workflow, and decision pressure. The goal is not to sell a package — it is to understand what is actually worth solving.

steps.title: Focused system design
steps.description: We define the target flow, scope boundaries, success criteria, and where AI adds leverage instead of complexity.

steps.title: Build and implementation
steps.description: We ship the system, integrate it into real workflows, and keep the loop tight enough to make product and technical decisions fast.

steps.title: Iteration with accountability
steps.description: After launch, the work continues through measured improvements, support, and direct communication instead of disappearing into a generic maintenance queue.

founder.sectionTitle: Work directly with the founder who helps shape and ship the system
founder.bio: No sales layer. No generic handoff. Rafał stays close to the decisions that define scope, delivery quality, and how the system actually lands inside your team.
supportingPoints: You talk directly to Rafał from the first conversation.
Product and technical trade-offs get decided fast, with context intact.
The work stays grounded in delivery, not in a brochure version of AI transformation.
founder.availability: If we work together, the relationship starts as a direct founder conversation — not a qualification funnel.
founder.quote: The goal of the first call is simple: help you see the real next move, whether or not we end up building it together.

products.title: Start with the product or workflow that solves today's bottleneck
products.subtitle: Some teams want us to build. Some want to improve an existing system. Others want to learn the exact AI workflow behind faster delivery. These examples help you choose the right path.

items.q: What kind of work is the best fit for AwesomeWorks?
items.a: The best fit is a team that needs more than a prototype. We work on AI systems, internal tools, workflow automation, and productized experiences where strategy and implementation have to stay connected.

items.q: Who do we talk to during the process?
items.a: Rafał is your direct point of contact from the first call and stays involved in the important product and technical decisions throughout the engagement.

items.q: What happens on the first call?
items.a: The first call is about clarity. We look at the problem, current workflow, constraints, and what the most sensible next step is. It should feel useful even if we do not end up working together.
Projects are scoped individually. After the clarity call, we define the right shape of work — audit, prototype, implementation, or product build — and then prepare a tailored proposal.

items.q: Do you stay involved after launch?
items.a: Yes. The goal is not just to release something, but to make it work inside the team. That usually means iteration, support, and ongoing improvements after launch.
Python, TypeScript, Astro, React, LLM APIs, workflow automation, evaluation layers, and cloud infrastructure. The stack follows the problem, not the other way around.

final_cta.title: If the problem is real, the best next step is a direct conversation with Rafał.
final_cta.subtitle: Bring the messy version. The first call is there to sharpen the decision, not push you into a package.
points: No sales deck — just an honest working conversation.
If there is a fit, Rafał stays close to the important decisions.
If there is no fit yet, you still leave with a clearer next move.

contact.title: Talk to Rafał or send context async
contact.subtitle: Calendly is the fastest path if you want clarity quickly. If it is easier, send a short brief through the form and we will respond with the next step.
contact.founderCue: Your first conversation is with Rafał — founder of AwesomeWorks and the person accountable for the work.
contact.call_title: What the first call is for
contact.call_summary: This is not a generic agency intro. The goal is to understand the pressure point, sharpen the decision, and see whether the right next step is a build, a prototype, or CourseAI for your internal team.
call_points: Clarify the business problem and current workflow
Define the most sensible next step
Decide whether you need a build partner, a prototype, or CourseAI
contact.async_body: Send context, screenshots, or a short brief if you prefer to start asynchronously.
contact.response_body: If you send a form inquiry, expect a direct response that moves the conversation forward instead of a generic acknowledgment.
contact.form_message: Tell us what you are trying to solve
contact.form_success: Thanks! We'll get back to you soon.
